judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all three expectations with equivalent substance; Report 2 offers marginally more rigorous confirmation methodology (AST parse, dedicated refutation pass, repo-wide compensating-control sweep) but the two are comparable in meeting the stated expectations.

report_1.evidence: Cross-tenant data leak: the search query is not tenant-scoped.** File: `backend/app/appointments.py`, `search_appointments` (branch lines 10–21)... there is no `tenant_id` condition anywhere, and the `tenant_id` argument is never bound into the query.

report_2.evidence: Cross-tenant data exposure (PHI leak) — the new search path has no tenant filter.** Location: `backend/app/appointments.py`, `search_appointments` (lines 10–20 on the branch)... the function accepts `tenant_id` but never uses it.

report_1.evidence: Runtime breakage of the nightly export: `build_appointment_query` gained a required argument but an existing caller was not updated.** Files: `backend/app/db.py` line 7 (signature changed to `build_appointment_query(session, tenant_id, filters)` with no default) and `backend/app/exports.py` line 11 (`nightly_export` still calls `build_appointment_query(session, tenant_id)`).

report_2.evidence: The nightly export crashes with `TypeError` on every run after merge.** Location: `backend/app/db.py:7` — `build_appointment_query(session, tenant_id, filters)` now takes `filters` as a **required** positional parameter (no default) — versus `backend/app/exports.py:9`, where `nightly_export` still calls `build_appointment_query(session, tenant_id)` with two arguments.

report_1.evidence: Today this is not exploitable through the HTTP endpoint, and I confirm that precisely: `backend/app/schemas.py` defines `AppointmentStatus` as a `str` Enum with only `booked`/`cancelled`/`completed`, and FastAPI rejects any other value for the `status: AppointmentStatus` query parameter before the handler runs.

report_2.evidence: this is **not exploitable over HTTP today** — `backend/app/api.py` types the parameter as `status: AppointmentStatus`, and `backend/app/schemas.py` defines that enum with exactly three fixed values (`booked`/`cancelled`/`completed`); FastAPI rejects anything else with a 422 before the handler runs.
